title: Supply Chain Disruption Risk Assessment
description: Industrial-grade risk assessment tool for quantifying and mitigating supply chain disruption risks based on ISO 31000, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Supplier Reliability Score (0-100)
inputs.businessContext: Historical on-time delivery and quality performance of key suppliers. Higher score indicates better reliability.

businessContext_i18n.en: Historical on-time delivery and quality performance of key suppliers. Higher score indicates better reliability.

inputs.label: Inventory Buffer (Days of Cover)
inputs.businessContext: Average days of inventory held to absorb demand or supply shocks. Based on lean inventory principles.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average days of inventory held to absorb demand or supply shocks. Based on lean inventory principles.

inputs.label: Lead Time Variability (Coefficient of Variation)
inputs.businessContext: Standard deviation of lead time divided by mean lead time. Higher values indicate unpredictable supply.

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard deviation of lead time divided by mean lead time. Higher values indicate unpredictable supply.

inputs.label: Single Source Dependency (0-100%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of critical components sourced from a single supplier. High dependency increases risk.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of critical components sourced from a single supplier. High dependency increases risk.

inputs.label: Geopolitical Risk Index (0-100)
inputs.businessContext: Composite index of political instability, trade restrictions, and regional conflicts affecting supply chain.

businessContext_i18n.en: Composite index of political instability, trade restrictions, and regional conflicts affecting supply chain.

inputs.label: Demand Volatility (Coefficient of Variation)
inputs.businessContext: Variability in customer demand over time. High volatility strains capacity and inventory.

businessContext_i18n.en: Variability in customer demand over time. High volatility strains capacity and inventory.

inputs.label: Transportation Disruption Probability (0-1)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated likelihood of a major transportation disruption (port closure, strike, etc.) in the next quarter.

businessContext_i18n.en: Estimated likelihood of a major transportation disruption (port closure, strike, etc.) in the next quarter.

inputs.label: Quality Defect Rate (ppm)
inputs.businessContext: Parts per million defective from incoming inspection. Six Sigma target is < 3.4 ppm.

businessContext_i18n.en: Parts per million defective from incoming inspection. Six Sigma target is < 3.4 ppm.

inputs.label: Cyber Risk Score (0-100)
inputs.businessContext: Assessment of cybersecurity vulnerabilities in supply chain IT systems. Higher score indicates higher risk.

businessContext_i18n.en: Assessment of cybersecurity vulnerabilities in supply chain IT systems. Higher score indicates higher risk.

inputs.label: Enable Advanced Hedging Strategies
inputs.businessContext: If true, applies financial and operational hedging models to mitigate currency and commodity risks.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, applies financial and operational hedging models to mitigate currency and commodity risks.

rules.message: Supplier reliability score must be between 0 and 100.
Inventory buffer days cannot be negative.
Single source dependency must be between 0 and 100%.
Geopolitical risk index must be between 0 and 100.
Transportation disruption probability must be between 0 and 1.
Cyber risk score must be between 0 and 100.
Advanced hedging is recommended only when single source dependency > 20% or geopolitical risk > 10.

thresholds.message: Critical disruption risk level. Immediate mitigation actions required.
Moderate disruption risk. Monitor and prepare contingency plans.
Low disruption risk. Continue standard risk management practices.

formulas.description: Normalized supplier risk based on reliability and single source dependency.
Risk from insufficient inventory buffer relative to lead time variability.
Combined external risks from geopolitical, transportation, and cyber sources.
Risk from demand volatility and quality defects (scaled to ppm).
Reduction in risk if advanced hedging is enabled (applies only if condition met).
Final disruption risk score clamped between 0 and 100.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Single Source Dependency Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated financial loss (in $) due to lack of alternative suppliers.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Quality Defect Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated cost of rework, scrap, and warranty from defects.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Transportation Disruption Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Expected loss from transportation interruptions per quarter.

suggestedActions.action: Implement supplier diversification program to reduce single source dependency below 30%.
Increase inventory buffer to at least 30 days to absorb lead time variability.
Initiate Six Sigma DMAIC project to reduce defect rate below 500 ppm.
Conduct comprehensive cybersecurity audit and implement NIST framework controls.
Enable advanced hedging strategies to mitigate currency and commodity risks.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Confidence-Adjusted Risk Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Risk score adjusted for data quality and completeness (0-100).
dataConfidenceAdjusted.value: primary_result * (1 - 0.1 * (1 - min(1, (supplier_reliability_score + inventory_buffer_days + lead_time_variability + single_source_dependency + geopolitical_risk_index + demand_volatility + transportation_disruption_probability + quality_defect_rate + cyber_risk_score) / 900)))
